items.q: What does the dopamine hypothesis of schizophrenia state?
items.correct: Positive symptoms are linked to excess dopamine activity in mesolimbic pathways.
items.misconception: Schizophrenia is caused by too little dopamine.
items.random_wrong: Schizophrenia is caused by too much serotonin.
items.why: Students reverse the direction. The original hypothesis was HYPERdopaminergia. The revised version notes hypoactivity in prefrontal areas for negative symptoms, but excess in mesolimbic for positive.

items.q: Asch's line study primarily demonstrates which type of conformity?
items.correct: Normative social influence (compliance to fit in).
items.misconception: Informational social influence (believing others know better).
items.why: Students assume participants genuinely believed the wrong line was correct. Asch's debriefs showed they knew it was wrong but conformed publicly to avoid rejection - this is normative, not informational.

items.q: According to Maslow, when can self-actualisation be achieved?
items.correct: Only after lower deficiency needs are largely met.
items.misconception: Anyone can self-actualise at any time through willpower.
items.why: Students miss the hierarchical structure. You cannot pursue growth needs while physiological, safety, love, and esteem needs are unmet - that's the whole point of a hierarchy.

items.q: Which manual is published by the WHO?
items.why: Correct answer: ICD. “DSM” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: For a depression diagnosis, symptoms typically last at least:
items.why: Correct answer: 2 weeks. “2 days” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Roughly how many DSM symptoms must be present to diagnose major depression?
items.why: Exact answer: 5. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
